summarization.b: A polar chart displaying the intensity distribution of sapphire and PbI2 reflections in reciprocal space.
summarization.c: Spectra charts showing the intensity of reflections as a function of angle for different crystal planes.

e.question: Are there 30°/90° domains inPbI2 grown over sapphire?
e.answer: In-plane XRD measurements confirmed the in-plane alignment, the absence of 30°/90° domains and the presence of some non-epitaxial domains

a.question: Can PbI2 be grown epitaxially over sapphire?

d.question: What suggests disorder in the PbI2 films?
d.answer: The relatively broad peaks in β (FWHM 13°, figure (c)) and α scans (FWHM 9°, figure (d)) extracted from the in-plane pole figure suggest disorder in both the in-plane and out-of-plane orientations, respectively.

b.question: Why was the pole figure measured at 25.95° ?
b.answer: The pole figure measured at 25.95° 2θ corresponds to the asymmetric (1 0 \bar(1) 1)PbI2 reflection but is also close enough to the asymmetric (0 1 \bar(1) 2)sapphire reflection to yield information on the film/substrate registration. Three sharp peaks over a 360° rotation were observed for the sapphire reflection, whereas six broader peaks were seen for PbI2, both being as expected for a single in-plane orientation considering the symmetry of the reflections. The alignment of the (1 0 \bar(1) 1)PbI2 and (0 1 \bar(1) 2)sapphire reflections corresponds to the (0 0 1) [2 \bar(11) 0]PbI2 ‖ (0 0 1)[2 \bar(11) 0]Al2O3 epitaxial relation, i.e. parallel alignment of all the unit cell axes of PbI2 and sapphire as expected from the relatively small lattice mismatch between the film and the substrate.
